caption:
Stop scattering isinstance() checks everywhere.

Python type guards narrow types automatically. Your IDE knows the type after the check. Zero runtime cost.

Which pattern do you use? A or B?

#python #typing #typeguard #coding #programming #developer #backend #pythontips #cleancode #codingtips

slides.title: Which type check is better?
slides.problem: Narrow a union type safely at runtime

a.label: isinstance + assert

b.label: TypeGuard

slides.title: B wins. Your IDE thanks you.

verdicts.title: Works but doesn't compose
verdicts.text: Every function repeats the same isinstance checks. The type narrowing only works inside that specific if block.

verdicts.title: Reusable type narrowing
verdicts.text: Define it once. Use it everywhere. Your editor and type checker know the type after the guard returns True.

slides.takeaway: TypeGuard makes isinstance checks composable and type-safe.

slides.title: TypeGuard in real code.
slides.sub: Filter lists, validate API responses, narrow unions.

items.text: Return TypeGuard[T] from a bool function
IDE narrows type after guard returns True
Works with isinstance, hasattr, any check
